intro: Lasiandra hieracioides, also known as Hieracium, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Lasiandra hieracioides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lasiandra hieracioides is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to alleviate symptoms of fever, inflammation, and digestive disorders.
